summary: Iteration 33: Public Endpoints & Floating Clock Button - Backend 19/19 passed (100%), Frontend all tests passed. QR and KDS public endpoints work without auth. Floating clock button visible and functional. ONE BUG FOUND: /attendance/clock endpoint blocked by router-level guard.

critical.issue: Router-level guard blocks public access. The attendance router has `router = APIRouter(dependencies=[Depends(require_feature('workforce'))])` which requires JWT auth for ALL endpoints including /attendance/clock which should be public (PIN-based auth only)
critical.fix: Remove router-level dependency and add require_feature('workforce') only to endpoints that need it (GET /attendance, GET /attendance/live, PUT /attendance/{id}/flag-resolve). Leave POST /attendance/clock without auth guard.

action_items: FIX CRITICAL: Remove router-level guard from attendance.py and add require_feature('workforce') only to endpoints that need auth (not /attendance/clock)
critical_code_review_comments: BUG: /app/backend/routers/attendance.py line 9 has router-level guard that blocks the public /attendance/clock endpoint
QR public endpoints correctly check features backward-compatibly (line 103-107 in qr_menu.py)
KDS public endpoints (verify-pin, public/orders, public/stats) correctly have NO auth guard
FloatingClockButton component correctly checks hasFeature('workforce') before rendering
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 33 verified: (1) QR public endpoints GET/POST /api/qr/{restaurant_id}/{table_hash} work without auth, (2) KDS public endpoints verify-pin, public/orders, public/stats work without auth, (3) KDS auth endpoints still require auth+feature, (4) Floating clock button visible for SKAdmin with workforce enabled, (5) PIN pad dialog opens correctly. BUG FOUND: /attendance/clock endpoint requires JWT auth due to router-level guard but should be public (PIN-based). Test PIN for clock: user account has pos_pin=1111.

kds_verify_pin.evidence: POST /api/kds/verify-pin returns 200 with kds_token without auth

kds_auth_protected.evidence: GET /api/kds/orders returns 403 without auth, 200 with auth

attendance_my_status.evidence: GET /api/attendance/my-status returns 200 for both admin and staff users

attendance_clock.evidence: POST /api/attendance/clock returns 403 without auth due to router-level guard - should be public

floating_clock_button.evidence: Button visible bottom-right on dashboard, POS, orders pages with data-testid='floating-clock-btn'

floating_clock_pinpad.evidence: PIN pad dialog opens with data-testid='floating-clock-dialog' and keys fclock-key-0 through fclock-key-9

pos_page.evidence: POS Terminal loads with product grid, categories, order panel

orders_page.evidence: Order History loads with order cards, filter tabs

sidebar_workforce_items.evidence: Sidebar shows Shift Scheduler, Attendance, Timesheets for SKAdmin

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: Line 9 of /app/backend/routers/attendance.py has `router = APIRouter(dependencies=[Depends(require_feature('workforce'))])` which applies the workforce feature guard to ALL endpoints in the router, including /attendance/clock which should be public (PIN-based auth)
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Remove router-level dependency. Add require_feature('workforce') individually to endpoints that need it: GET /attendance, GET /attendance/live, GET /attendance/my-status, PUT /attendance/{id}/flag-resolve. Leave POST /attendance/clock without any auth guard since it uses PIN-based authentication.
